sections.paragraph:
συγκαλέω 1 aor. συνεκάλεσα, mid. συνεκαλεσάμην. (Act.: Hom. et al.; SIG 1185, 15f; PLond V, 1711, 53; LXX; JosAs 21:7 cod. A [p. 71, 25 Bat.]; Jos., Ant. 7, 363; 18, 279. Mid.: Hdt. 2, 160, 2; 2 Macc 15:31.)

① to summon pers. to come together, summon, act. foll. by acc. (Arrian, Anab. Alex. 6, 22, 2 ξυγκαλεῖν τὸ πλῆθος) Mk 15:16; Lk 15:6, 9; Ac 5:21; 1 Cl 43:5; Hs 5, 2, 11.

② to call to one’s side, summon, mid. (on relation to act. s. B-D-F §316, 1) foll. by the acc. Lk 9:1; 15:6 v.l., 9 v.l.; 23:13; Ac 5:21 D; 10:24; 13:7 D; 28:17; Hs 5, 2, 11 v.l.—M-M s.v. συνκαλέω. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

act. act. = active

foll. foll. = followed, following

acc. acc. = accusative

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 951
